channel.title: Snmp on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Snmp on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted SNMP Collectors: snmp_exporter vs SNMPcollector vs Telegraf 2026
item.description:
Why Self-Host Your SNMP Data Collection

Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) remains the universal language for monitoring routers, switches, UPS systems, printers, servers, and industrial equipment. Whether you are tracking interface bandwidth on a core switch, monitoring temperature sensors in a data center, or collecting CPU and disk metrics from legacy servers, SNMP is the protocol that powers it all.

item.title: Netdisco vs LibreNMS vs OpenNetAdmin: Best Network Discovery Tools 2026
item.description:
Why Self-Host Network Discovery and Topology Mapping

If you manage more than a handful of switches, routers, or access points, you need a reliable way to know what devices are on your network, how they are connected, and which ports are active. Cloud-based network management tools require sending SNMP credentials and topology data to external servers — a security risk most organizations cannot accept.
